items.title: O(n²) similarity blow-up
items.scenario: A retrieval prototype loops over every (chunk, chunk) pair to compute similarity for clustering.
items.explanation: At 1k chunks it's 1M comparisons (fine), at 100k chunks it's 10 billion (impossible). You need ANN indexing (HNSW, IVF) or batched matrix ops.

items.title: O(n) per-token loop becomes O(n²)
items.scenario: A streaming post-processor scans the entire response string after every new token to fix formatting.
items.explanation: Each scan is O(n); doing it n times is O(n²). Track an offset and only inspect new bytes for O(n) overall.

items.title: O(n log n) sort to enable O(log n) lookups
items.scenario: You receive a million (timestamp, log_line) records and need fast range queries.
items.explanation: Pay an O(n log n) sort once, then each range query is O(log n + k). Without the sort each query is O(n).

items.title: Amortized O(1) in practice
items.scenario: Streaming millions of tokens into a Python list with `tokens.append(t)`.
items.explanation: Most appends are O(1); occasional resizes are O(n) but spread thinly. End-to-end behaves linearly — exactly amortized analysis predicts.
